Finalized Python function:
```python
def Place_Short_Horizontal_Element_On_Smart_Conveyor_by_Rob2_Gripper(
    el_name: str = None,
    X: float = None,
    Y: float = None,
    L: float = None,
    H: float = None,
) -> float:
    # Moving Conveyor
    Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) - L / 2 + 2.625101)

    # It Calculates the Conveyor Joint Value where the Stud is in 0 Position
    Conv_Curr_Loc: float = -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275

    # Robot 2 Pre Place Movement
    Examiner: bool = Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                    target_pose= [X + 1.13259,
                                  0,
                                  H + 1.18936],
                    target_orientation= [0, -1, 0, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)

    if Examiner == True:
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        # Robot 2 Place Movement
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X + 1.13259,
                                    0,
                                    H + 0.98936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -1, 0, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        # Detach
        Robot_2.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                            detaching_object_name= el_name)
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= el_name)

    else:
        print("Bottom Silling Plate Place location is out of Robot#2 Reach !")
        print("Manual Action Needed to Place the Stud")
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                    target_pose= [X + H / 2 + 1.12649,
                                  0,
                                  H + 1.18936],
                    target_orientation= [0, -ev, 0, ev],
                    update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_2.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                            detaching_object_name= el_name) 

        # Stud
        # [ev, 0, ev, 0]
        dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/"+el_name).GetAttribute("physics:rigidBodyEnabled").Set(True)
        object = dc.get_rigid_body("/world/obstacles/"+el_name)
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)

        # Create new position
        New_Loc = Gf.Vec3d(object_pose.p[0], object_pose.p[1], H / 2 + 0.89546)
        # Create a valid quaternion and rotation
        quat = Gf.Quatd(ev, 0, 1, 0)  # Ensure this follows (real, x, y, z)
        New_Or = Gf.Rotation(quat)
        # Convert quaternion to required format
        quat_as_tuple = (New_Or.GetQuat().GetReal(), *New_Or.GetQuat().GetImaginary())
        # Create a Transform object
        new_transform = _dynamic_control.Transform()
        new_transform.p.x = New_Loc[0]
        new_transform.p.y = New_Loc[1]
        new_transform.p.z = New_Loc[2]
        new_transform.r.w = quat_as_tuple[0]  # real part
        new_transform.r.x = quat_as_tuple[1]
        new_transform.r.y = quat_as_tuple[2]
        new_transform.r.z = quat_as_tuple[3]
        # Apply transformation
        dc.set_rigid_body_pose(object, new_transform)

        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/"+el_name).GetAttribute("physics:rigidBodyEnabled").Set(False)
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= el_name)

    # Back To Home
    Robot_2.move_to_home()

    return Conv_Curr_Loc
```

tool_arguments.summary: Commands Robot 2 to drop a short horizontal element that is currently held by its “tool0” gripper onto the smart conveyor. The routine first positions the conveyor so the element’s longitudinal centre aligns with the conveyor’s zero station, then tries to move the robot above the target location, lowers the element into final contact, releases the grasp, re-enables gravity, attaches the part to the conveyor fixture, and finally returns the robot to its home pose. The function outputs the conveyor joint value that realigns the conveyor’s zero reference to the element’s Y coordinate.
procedure_steps: Calculate the joint displacement required to bring the smart conveyor’s Joint_1 so that the element’s longitudinal centre (Y – L⁄2) coincides with the conveyor reference. Command Smart_Conv.render_exec to apply this motion.
Derive Conv_Curr_Loc – the conveyor joint position that represents the element’s zero (centre) location – from the provided Y value and store it for later return.
Ask Robot 2 for a collision-aware motion plan (pre-place pose) that positions TCP “tool0” at [X + 1.13259, 0, H + 1.18936] with quaternion [0, -1, 0, 0]. The plan result is stored in Examiner.

If a valid pre-place plan exists (Examiner is True):
 a. Execute the pre-place trajectory with Robot_2.render_exec.
 b. Plan a final placing motion to [X + 1.13259, 0, H + 0.98936] using the same orientation, while ignoring the smart conveyor and world obstacles and applying a grasp-approach cost metric.
 c. Execute this place motion.
 d. Command Robot 2 to open the gripper (eef_detach) and release the element identified by el_name.
 e. Re-enable gravity for the detached element in the physics scene.
 f. Call Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv so the released element is now considered part of (attached to) the moving conveyor.

If no valid pre-place plan exists (Examiner is False):
 a. Notify the operator that the target pose is outside Robot 2’s workspace via two print statements.
 b. Attempt an alternative approach: plan and execute a motion to [X + H⁄2 + 1.12649, 0, H + 1.18936] with orientation [0, -ev, 0, ev].
 c. Detach the element from the gripper.
 d. Temporarily enable physics on the element and query its current rigid-body pose via the dynamic control interface.
 e. Compute a new pose that lifts the element to Z = H⁄2 + 0.89546 while retaining its X/Y position, create a corresponding quaternion, build a Transform message, and set this pose on the rigid body.
 f. Disable rigid-body simulation again, re-enable gravity, and attach the element to the conveyor.
Command Robot 2 to return to its predefined home position with move_to_home.
Return Conv_Curr_Loc so downstream logic knows the conveyor joint value corresponding to the element’s placement.

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Unique identifier of the element that is currently grasped and must be detached and attached to the conveyor.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: Target X-coordinate of the element’s placement, used when planning the robot’s approach and final pose.

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: Target Y-coordinate of the element’s placement, also used to compute the conveyor’s Joint_1 displacement and Conv_Curr_Loc.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Overall length of the horizontal element; half of this value is considered when translating the conveyor to centre the element.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height (thickness) of the element above the world reference, used to compute the robot TCP’s Z-level during approach and placement and to adjust the element’s final rigid-body pose if manual placement is required.

tool_arguments.returns: Float value Conv_Curr_Loc – the computed Joint_1 position of the smart conveyor that aligns the element’s centre with the conveyor’s zero reference.
operational_notes: Function assumes the element is already rigidly attached to Robot 2’s gripper (tool0).
If the initial motion planning fails (Examiner == False) the function falls back to an alternate pose, issues operator warnings, and manually relocates the element via direct physics manipulation.

An external variable 'ev' is referenced for orientation in the fallback branch but is not defined within the function; executing that branch will raise a NameError unless 'ev' exists in the surrounding scope.
All moves are executed in simulation through Robot_2.render_exec; no explicit real-world motion commands or feedback loops are present.
After placement, Robot 2 always returns to its home configuration regardless of success or fallback path.

  "function_name": "Nail_Short_Horizontal_Element_by_Rob2_NailGun",
  "parameters": [

      "name": "push_to_nail",
      "annotation": "float",
      "default": "0.01",
      "known_semantics": "Linear push offset used to advance the nail-gun TCP during a nailing motion."
    },
    {
      "name": "el_pose",

def Nail_Short_Horizontal_Element_by_Rob2_NailGun(
    push_to_nail: float = 0.01,
    el_pose: List[float] = None,
    el_dims: List[float] = None,
    conv_current_location: float = None,
) -> None:
    quat = R.from_euler('xyz', [(-(np.pi/2)+np.radians(SILL_NAILING_ANGLE)), 0, np.pi]).as_quat()
    quat[1] *= -1

    # [2.6109899999999997, -0.3533974596215561, 0.97594], Orientation (W,X,Y,Z): [-0.8660254037844386, 0.5000000000000001, 0.0, 0.0]
    # Right Nail
    if(conv_current_location-(el_dims[0]/2)-NAILING_CONV_TARGET < 0):
        print("Right Nail is Not Possible ! Due To Conveyor Reachability")
    else:
        Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', conv_current_location - el_dims[0] / 2 - 0.5)

        # Pre Nail
        Nail_Doable: bool = Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [el_pose[0] + 1.13259,
                                    el_dims[1] * 1.5 - 0.413397459621,
                                    el_dims[2] * 0.3 + 0.94546],
                        target_orientation= [quat[3], quat[0], quat[1], quat[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        
        if(Nail_Doable == True):

            Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()

            # Nail 1
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool1")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] + -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.866025403785,
                                        object_pose.p[2] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.5],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 1 Backward
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool1")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] - -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.866025403785,
                                        object_pose.p[2] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.5],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 2 Prep
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool1")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1],
                                        object_pose.p[2] + 0.4 * el_dims[2]],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=1))
            Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)
    

            # Nail 2
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool1")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] + -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.866025403785,
                                        object_pose.p[2] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.5],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 2 Backward
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool1")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] - -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.866025403785,
                                        object_pose.p[2] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.5],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            Robot_2.move_to_home(removing_primitives= ["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"])
        else:
            print("Right Nail is Not Possible ! Due To Robot NailGun Reachability")
```

tool_arguments.summary: Drives two horizontally-oriented nails into the right edge of a short element using Robot_2’s nail-gun TCP (tool1) after positioning the smart conveyor. The routine plans and executes an approach pose, a push-in stroke, a retraction, a vertical index to the second nail location, a second push-in/retraction cycle, and finally returns the robot to its home configuration.
procedure_steps: Calculate the desired tool orientation: convert fixed XYZ Euler angles (−90° + SILL_NAILING_ANGLE, 0°, 180°) to a quaternion and negate the Y component so the gun faces the element correctly.

Verify conveyor reachability for right-side nailing by checking whether (conv_current_location − el_dims[0]/2 − NAILING_CONV_TARGET) is non-negative; abort with a console message if the element cannot be brought within reach.
Command Smart_Conv.render_exec to translate Joint_1 to conv_current_location − el_dims[0]/2 − 0.5, placing the element at the calculated nailing position.

Plan a pre-nail approach for Robot_2.tool1 at X = el_pose[0] + 1.13259, Y = el_dims[1] * 1.5 − 0.413397459621, Z = el_dims[2] * 0.3 + 0.94546 with the computed orientation; continue only if the planner reports success.
Execute the pre-nail motion with Robot_2.render_exec, then acquire a dynamic control interface for live TCP feedback.

First nail push-in: obtain the current tool pose, compute a push offset based on element height and push_to_nail, and plan a linear descent along the gun axis while applying a grasp-approach cost metric and temporarily removing Smart_Conveyor and world/obstacles; execute the plan.
First nail retraction: read the updated tool pose and plan the mirrored retreat motion to the original depth; execute.
Second nail preparation: from the current pose, plan and execute a 0.4 * el_dims[2] upward move to index the gun to the second nailing height.
Second nail push-in and retraction: repeat the push/retract sequence described in steps 6 and 7 for the new height.
Command Robot_2.move_to_home, again removing Smart_Conveyor and world/obstacles, to leave the workstation in a safe, known state.

arguments.name: push_to_nail
arguments.description: Linear advance distance (in metres) added to the nominal penetration depth when pushing the nail-gun TCP into the element.

arguments.name: el_pose
arguments.description: World-frame pose of the element as a 6-DOF list; the X component is used to offset the robot’s pre-nail approach position.

arguments.name: el_dims
arguments.description: Element dimensions [length, width, height]; used to compute conveyor shift, lateral push distance, and vertical indexing between the two nail positions.

arguments.name: conv_current_location
arguments.description: Current Joint_1 position of the smart conveyor; informs reachability checks and determines the new conveyor set-point for nailing.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function performs motion planning and execution commands and exits silently unless reachability checks fail, in which case it prints an explanatory message before returning.
operational_notes: The function addresses only right-side nailing; left-side or alternate orientations are not handled.
If the initial conveyor reachability test fails, or if the pre-nail plan is infeasible, the robot does not move and an explanatory message is printed.
All robot motions are executed immediately after successful planning via Robot_2.render_exec; no additional verification, force sensing, or nail-firing signal is sent.
Throughout the nailing sequence the environment models 'Smart_Conveyor' and 'world/obstacles' are temporarily excluded from collision checking to allow close approaches.
A PoseCostMetric with a linear-axis approach constraint guides the push-in and retreat trajectories to maintain alignment along the nail axis.

def Place_Short_Vertical_Element_On_Smart_Conveyor_by_Rob1_Gripper(el_name: str = None,
        X: float = None,
        Y: float = None,
        L: float = None,
        H: float = None):
    # Moving Conveyor
    Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275)

    # # It Calculates the Conveyor Joint Value where the Stud is in 0 Position
    Conv_Curr_Loc: float = -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275
    Solver_Flag: bool = False
    Side_Selector: float = 1

    # Check if Positive Value is Suitable for Robot !
    if((-(Y - (OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)) + (SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET <= SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1)):
        Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.775)
        # Robot 1 Pre Place
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X - -(L / 2) + 0.632489,
                                        0.5,
                                        H + 1.08936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -ev, -ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        # Robot 1 Drop
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X - -(L / 2) + 0.632489,
                                        0.5,
                                        H + 0.98936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -ev, -ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "IRB6620_R2"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                            detaching_object_name= el_name)
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= el_name)

        # Robot 1 Drop
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X - -(L / 2) + 0.632489,
                                        0.5,
                                        H + 1.08936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -ev, -ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "IRB6620_R2"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_1.move_to_home()

        # Robot_1_Do_Side_Nail(push_to_nail= PUSH_TO_NAIL_OFFSET, H= H, Side_Selector= 1)
        Side_Selector = 1

        Solver_Flag = True

    # Check if Negative Value is Suitable for Robot !
    if((-(Y - (OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)) + (SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)-NAILING_CONV_TARGET*2 >= 0) and Solver_Flag == False):
        Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) + 1.275)
        # Robot 1 Pre Place
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X + -(L / 2) + 1.632691,
                                        -1.0,
                                        H + 1.08936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -ev, ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        # Robot 1 Drop
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X + -(L / 2) + 1.632691,
                                        -1.0,
                                        H + 0.98936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -ev, ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "IRB6620_R2"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                            detaching_object_name= el_name)
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= el_name)

        # Robot 1 Drop
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X + -(L / 2) + 1.632691,
                                        -1.0,
                                        H + 1.08936],
                        target_orientation= [0, -ev, ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "IRB6620_R2"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_1.move_to_home()

        # Changing Is_TCP to true for the condition that cripple is being dropped in Y = -nail_offset (to set the nailgun's target to -2*nail_offset rather than nail_offset)
        # why? because on y = -nail_offset the robot's nailgun will hit the links, so we have to push it further !
        # Robot_1_Do_Side_Nail(push_to_nail= PUSH_TO_NAIL_OFFSET, H= H, Side_Selector= -1, Is_TCP= True)
        Side_Selector = -1

        Solver_Flag = True

    # Check if it's reachable for Robot 1 to Place the Cripple
    if((-(Y - (OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)) + (SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET > SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1) and 
        (-(Y - (OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)) + (SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)-NAILING_CONV_TARGET*2 < 0) and
        Solver_Flag == False):
        # Robot 1 Can't Reach The Place Location with the Placing Orientation
        print("Robot 1 Needs Human's Help to Place the Top Cripple")
        # [0, -1, 0, 0]

        # Robot 1 Reach
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                        target_pose= [X + 1.13259,
                                        -(L / 2) + 0.500101,
                                        H + 1.08936],
                        target_orientation= [0, 1, 0, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        # Relocating the Stud into its Location
        # [90, 0, 90]
        # [0.5, 0.5, -0.5, 0.5]
        Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                            detaching_object_name= el_name) 

        dc = _dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physics:rigidBodyEnabled").Set(True)
        object = dc.get_rigid_body("/world/obstacles/" + el_name)
        object_pose = dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)

        # Create new position
        New_Loc = Gf.Vec3d(object_pose.p[0], object_pose.p[1], H / 2 + 0.89546)

        # Define the new orientation quaternion directly
        quat_as_tuple = (0.5, 0.5, -0.5, 0.5)  # (real, x, y, z)

        # Create a Transform object
        new_transform = _dynamic_control.Transform()
        new_transform.p.x = New_Loc[0]
        new_transform.p.y = New_Loc[1]
        new_transform.p.z = New_Loc[2]
        new_transform.r.w = quat_as_tuple[0]  # real part
        new_transform.r.x = quat_as_tuple[1]
        new_transform.r.y = quat_as_tuple[2]
        new_transform.r.z = quat_as_tuple[3]

        # Apply transformation
        dc.set_rigid_body_pose(object, new_transform)

        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physics:rigidBodyEnabled").Set(False)
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name=el_name)

        Robot_1.move_to_home()

        if (OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)- Y +(SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET > SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1:
            Side_Selector = -1

        elif (OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)- Y +(SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)-(NAILING_CONV_TARGET*2) < 0:
            Side_Selector = 1
    
    return Side_Selector
```

tool_arguments.summary: Positions a short vertical framing element ("cripple") onto the smart conveyor.  Robot_1, equipped with TCP "tool0", first drives the conveyor to the Y–aligned loading slot, then attempts to place and release the element from one of two gripper approach directions.  After detaching the element and re–enabling gravity, the element is rigidly attached to the conveyor.  The routine finishes by homing the robot and returns which side of the panel will later be accessible for nailing.
procedure_steps: Translate the smart conveyor’s Joint_1 so that the requested Y-coordinate of the element aligns with the nominal zero position on the conveyor; store the commanded joint offset in "Conv_Curr_Loc" and initialise helper flags ("Solver_Flag" and default "Side_Selector" = 1).

Branch 1 – positive Y reachability: If the computed conveyor offset keeps the nailing target within the conveyor’s positive range of motion, move the conveyor a small extra distance, plan and execute Robot_1 motions that:  a) move above the deposit point,  b) descend to the release height,  c) detach the element from the gripper (disabling its gravity lock), and  d) command the conveyor to attach the freed element.  Robot_1 then retreats to a safe height and returns home.  "Side_Selector" is set to 1 and "Solver_Flag" to True.

Branch 2 – negative Y reachability: If the first condition fails but the target lies within the conveyor’s negative range of motion, perform a mirrored sequence with a negative Y approach (tool frame rotated about Y).  After the same detach–and–attach sequence, Robot_1 returns home, "Side_Selector" is set to -1, and "Solver_Flag" becomes True.

Branch 3 – human-assist fallback: If neither positive nor negative approach is reachable, print a help message and move Robot_1 to a hand-off pose.  The element is then released, converted to a dynamic rigid body, manually re-positioned in simulation through a dynamic-control Transform to the desired height and orientation, re-fixed to the conveyor, and gravity re-enabled.  Robot_1 finally returns home and sets "Side_Selector" to −1 or 1 based on which side will be reachable for subsequent nailing.
Return the final value of "Side_Selector" to indicate the element’s accessible side for downstream nailing tasks (+1 for the robot’s positive Y side, −1 for the negative side).

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Unique identifier of the element to be placed; used for end-effector detachment, physics property modification, and conveyor attachment.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: Target X coordinate of the element’s placement position in simulation/world units.

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: Target Y coordinate along the conveyor; drives conveyor joint positioning and reachability checks.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Length of the element; used to offset robot approach poses in X and Y.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the element; used to compute vertical approach, release, and final placement elevations.

tool_arguments.returns: Integer scalar (1 or −1).  A value of 1 means the element was or should be nailed from the robot’s positive Y side; −1 indicates the negative Y side.  The value is decided according to which placement orientation was feasible or, in the fallback path, by residual reachability.
operational_notes: Relies on global constants (e.g., OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH, SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1, NAILING_CONV_TARGET) to evaluate reachability and conveyor motion.
Assumes external helpers (Smart_Conv, Robot_1, PoseCostMetric, _dynamic_control, test) are already instantiated and valid in the execution context.
Element gravity is re-enabled before attaching it to the conveyor to allow correct physics simulation.
No explicit error handling is implemented; failure to achieve any placement branch results in returning the default Side_Selector value (1).
Robot_1 always returns to its predefined home pose before the function finishes.

```python
def Place_Short_Vertical_Element_On_Smart_Conveyor_by_Rob2_Gripper(el_name: str = None,
        X: float = None,
        Y: float = None,
        L: float = None,
        H: float = None):
    # Moving Conveyor
    Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275)

    # Home
    Robot_2.move_to_home()

    # Pre Place1
    Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                    target_pose= [X - L / 2 + 1.577611,
                                  0,
                                  H + 1.03936],
                    target_orientation= [0, -ev, -ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)
    Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Pre Place2
    Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                    target_pose= [X - L / 2 + 1.477611,
                                  0,
                                  H + 0.88936],
                    target_orientation= [0, -ev, -ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                    orientational_restriction=torch.tensor([1,1,1], dtype=torch.float32))
    Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)
    
    Robot_2.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                        detaching_object_name= el_name)
    # Enabling Gravity For The Stud
    test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/" + el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
    Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= el_name)

    # Post Place
    Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                    target_pose= [X - L / 2 + 1.577611,
                                  0,
                                  H + 1.03936],
                    target_orientation= [0, -ev, -ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                    orientational_restriction=torch.tensor([1,1,1], dtype=torch.float32))
    Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    Robot_2.move_to_home()

    # Adding Nail Targets For BPL !

    # Conveyor Move For Placement
    Side_Selector: float = 0

    Side_Selector = 1
    if ((OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)- Y +(SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET > SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1):
        Side_Selector = -1

    Side_Selector = -1
    if ((OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)- Y +(SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET < 0):
        Side_Selector = 1

    # # Saving Joint Location For Nailing
    Smart_Conv._nail_poses.append(((OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH/2)- Y +(SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET*Side_Selector, Side_Selector))
```

tool_arguments.summary: Positions the smart conveyor, guides Robot_2 through a two-stage approach to place a short vertical element, detaches the element, re-enables its gravity, secures it to the conveyor, retreats to a safe pose, and records the conveyor’s joint pose for a later nailing task.
procedure_steps: Command Smart_Conv to translate its first joint so the conveyor deck aligns with the requested Y location (offset by the constants 1.8288 m and 2.275 m).
Return Robot_2 with tool ‘tool0’ to its predefined home configuration to ensure a known, collision-free start state.

Plan and execute a first pre-placement motion (‘Pre Place1’): move the TCP to a pose above the target drop point at X − L⁄2 + 1.577611 in X, 0 in Y, and H + 1.03936 in Z with the specified quaternion orientation; update the world state in the planner and execute the trajectory.

Plan and execute a second, lower pre-placement motion (‘Pre Place2’) to X − L⁄2 + 1.477611, 0, H + 0.88936 with identical orientation; during planning, temporarily ignore the Smart_Conveyor and world/obstacles primitives and constrain orientation with a torch tensor [1,1,1].
Command Robot_2 to open its gripper on tool ‘tool0’ and detach the object named el_name, leaving the element supported by the conveyor.

Enable physics on the released element by clearing its ‘physxRigidBody:disableGravity’ flag and immediately attach the element to the conveyor using Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv so it moves with the belt.

Plan and execute a retreat motion (‘Post Place’) that lifts the TCP back to the earlier, higher pre-placement pose while still ignoring the conveyor and obstacles and respecting the orientation constraint.
Return Robot_2 to the home position, freeing the work envelope for subsequent operations.

Determine which lateral side of the conveyor will be used for the upcoming nailing operation by computing a Side_Selector sign based on the element’s Y position relative to the conveyor’s motion range.
Append a tuple containing the chosen joint position and Side_Selector into Smart_Conv._nail_poses, storing the target needed for later nailing actions.

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: String identifier of the element being placed; used for detaching the object from the robot, enabling its gravity, and attaching it to the conveyor.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: World-frame X coordinate of the element’s reference point, used to compute robot approach and retreat poses.

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: World-frame Y coordinate corresponding to the desired conveyor position; also influences side selection for future nailing operations.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Length of the element; half of this value is subtracted from X to align the robot TCP with the element’s placement edge.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the element; added to fixed offsets to generate safe Z-heights for the robot’s approach and retreat trajectories.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function performs motion planning/execution, object detachment, physics property modification, conveyor attachment, and bookkeeping as side effects.
operational_notes: Requires Robot_2 with TCP ‘tool0’ to be holding the element identified by el_name at invocation.
Assumes global constants (e.g., OVERALL_PANEL_LENGTH, SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1, NAILING_CONV_TARGET, ev) and modules (Smart_Conv, test) are defined and accessible.
The two pre-placement waypoints provide a staged descent that avoids collisions; the same higher waypoint is reused for the post-placement retreat.
Gravity is disabled for the element while grasped and explicitly re-enabled after detachment to let the element settle on the conveyor.
The function records a future nailing pose but does not execute any nailing; it only prepares data for later processes.

Determine which lateral side of the conveyor will be used for the upcoming nailing operation by computing a Side_Selector sign based on the element’s Y position relative to the conveyor’s motion range.
Append a tuple containing the chosen joint position and Side_Selector into Smart_Conv._nail_poses, storing the target needed for later nailing actions.

arguments.description: Length of the element; half of this value is subtracted from X to align the robot TCP with the element’s placement edge.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the element; added to fixed offsets to generate safe Z-heights for the robot’s approach and retreat trajectories.

The two pre-placement waypoints provide a staged descent that avoids collisions; the same higher waypoint is reused for the post-placement retreat.
Gravity is disabled for the element while grasped and explicitly re-enabled after detachment to let the element settle on the conveyor.
The function records a future nailing pose but does not execute any nailing; it only prepares data for later processes.

Finalized Python function:
```python
def Pass_8ft_Element_G2S(el_name: str = None,
        # 8ft For Now
        L: float = 2.4384,
        H: float = None):
    # Moving Conveyor To Start Point
    Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', 0)

    # We Consider this As a Constant Point Regardless of the Dimensions of the Passing L_U
    R2_LU_Pass_Loc: List[float] = [2.81634, 1.25053, 1.35738]

    Length_Diff_Term: float = L - 0.500021000104

    Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool0",
                    target_pose= R2_LU_Pass_Loc,
                    target_orientation= [-0.6123661 ,  0.6123697 ,  0.35354862,  0.35357386],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["world/obstacles"],)
    Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Pre Passing
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [R2_LU_Pass_Loc[0] - Length_Diff_Term * 0.866025403785,
                                  R2_LU_Pass_Loc[1] + H / 2 + 0.173499901277,
                                  R2_LU_Pass_Loc[2] + Length_Diff_Term * 0.5 - 0.1],
                    target_orientation= [0.96593, 0, 0.25882, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Passing
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [R2_LU_Pass_Loc[0] - Length_Diff_Term * 0.866025403785,
                                  R2_LU_Pass_Loc[1] + H / 2 + 0.173499901277,
                                  R2_LU_Pass_Loc[2] + Length_Diff_Term * 0.5 - 0.06],
                    target_orientation= [0.96593, 0, 0.25882, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["IRB6620_R2"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Attach
    Robot_2.eef_detach(tool_name="tool0",
                        detaching_object_name= el_name)
    # Isaac Attach is Not Working Cause the OmniGraph Attacher Coordination is Outside of the Stud for Suction !!
    Robot_1.eef_attach(tool_name="tool1",
                       attaching_object_name=el_name)
    
    Robot_2.move_to_home()
    Robot_1.move_to_home()
```

tool_arguments.summary: Transfers an ~2.4 m (8 ft) long structural element from Robot_2’s end-effector to Robot_1’s end-effector at a fixed hand-off location after first positioning a conveyor.
procedure_steps: Command the Smart_Conv conveyor’s Joint_1 axis to the zero position, ensuring the conveyor is clear for the incoming hand-off trajectory.
Define R2_LU_Pass_Loc – the global Cartesian pass-off point used regardless of the element’s actual size.
Compute Length_Diff_Term as the difference between the provided element length (L) and a 0.500021 m reference. This value is later used to offset Robot_1’s approach and pass poses.

Robot_2 performs a motion plan with tool0 toward R2_LU_Pass_Loc with a specified quaternion orientation, requests world-model updating, and temporarily removes the “world/obstacles” collision primitive.
Execute the planned Robot_2 trajectory by rendering it in the simulator (Robot_2.render_exec). Robot_2 now holds the element at the designated pass-off location.

Robot_1 generates a pre-pass (approach) plan with tool1 to a pose offset from R2_LU_Pass_Loc by (−0.8660·Length_Diff_Term, +H/2 + 0.1735, +0.5·Length_Diff_Term − 0.10). The orientation is fixed to [0.96593, 0, 0.25882, 0].
Execute the Robot_1 pre-pass motion.

Robot_1 plans a final pass-off motion to close the 40 mm gap along the local Z-axis (offset −0.04 m) while reusing the same orientation; it removes the “IRB6620_R2” collision primitive and applies a grasp-approach cost metric generated by PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric.
Execute the Robot_1 pass-off motion, aligning its end-effector with the element held by Robot_2.
Detach the element (el_name) from Robot_2’s tool0 using Robot_2.eef_detach, releasing the payload.
Attach the same element to Robot_1’s tool1 using Robot_1.eef_attach, completing the physical transfer.
Command both Robot_2 and Robot_1 to return to their respective home positions via move_to_home, clearing the workcell for subsequent operations.

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Identifier of the element to be detached from Robot_2’s end-effector and attached to Robot_1’s end-effector.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Total length of the element in metres (default 2.4384 m ≈ 8 ft); used to calculate approach offsets for Robot_1.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the element in metres; influences the vertical offset when Robot_1 approaches the pass-off point.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function completes after both robots have returned home; it does not explicitly return a value.
operational_notes: Assumes the element identified by el_name is already attached to Robot_2’s tool0 when the function is invoked.
Requires the fixed pass-off location R2_LU_Pass_Loc to be reachable by both robots with the specified tools and orientations.

Temporarily removes specified collision primitives (“world/obstacles”, “IRB6620_R2”) during planning to avoid false collision detections in the motion planner; these primitives are expected to be restored automatically by the underlying system after execution.
Relies on PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric to bias the final hand-off approach along the tool’s linear axis for a straight-line insertion over the last 40 mm.
Executed entirely in simulation/rendered environment; actual physical execution depends on the backend implementation of render_exec and eef attach/detach calls.

Finalized Python function:
```python
def Place_8ft_Vertical_Element_On_Smart_Conveyor_by_Rob1_Suction(el_name: str = None,
        X: float = None,
        Y: float = None,
        Z: float = None,
        L: float = None,
        W: float = None,
        H: float = None):
    # Moving Conveyor
    Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275)

    Placement_Offset: float = 0.00565

    #Robot 1 Pre Place
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-X - L / 2 - Placement_Offset + 3.720990000208,
                                  -0.179599901277,
                                  Z + W / 2 + 1.02546],
                    target_orientation= [0, 1, 0, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)
    
    #Robot 1 Place
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-X - L / 2 - Placement_Offset + 3.720990000208,
                                  -0.179599901277,
                                  Z + W / 2 + 0.92546],
                    target_orientation= [0, 1, 0, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["world/obstacles", "Smart_Conveyor"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool1", detaching_object_name= el_name)
    test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/"+el_name).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
    Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= el_name, Enable_Gravity= False)
    # Enabling Colliders
    test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/"+el_name).GetAttribute("physics:collisionEnabled").Set(True)

    #Robot 1 Post Place
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-X - L / 2 - Placement_Offset + 3.720990000208,
                                  -0.179599901277,
                                  Z + W / 2 + 1.02546],
                    target_orientation= [0, 1, 0, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["world/obstacles", "Smart_Conveyor"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    Robot_1.move_to_home()
```

tool_arguments.summary: Positions Robot_1’s suction end-effector above a specified 8-foot vertical element, lowers it to the final set-down height, detaches the element, mounts it onto the smart conveyor at the requested X-, Y-, Z-location, re-enables physics on the element, retracts the robot to a safe height, and finally returns the robot to its home pose.
procedure_steps: Translate Smart_Conveyor Joint_1 so that its pallet aligns with the requested Y coordinate; the joint offset is computed as −(Y − 1.8288)+2.275.
Declare a constant vertical Placement_Offset (0.00565 m) used to slightly shift the robot’s X approach positions.
Pre-place approach: plan a motion for Robot_1 with tcp “tool1” to a hover pose directly above the element’s target location (Z elevated by 1.02546 m plus half the element width). Execute the plan.

Place motion: plan and execute a second motion that lowers the tool by 0.10 m (from +1.02546 m to +0.92546 m above half width) to bring the element into contact with the conveyor. Path planning employs a grasp-approach cost metric along the Z axis and temporarily removes the obstacle and conveyor primitives from collision checking.
Command the end-effector to release the element (eef_detach) and disable gravity flag on the element so it can be kinematically attached to the moving conveyor.
Call Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv to rigidly attach the released element to the conveyor while keeping gravity disabled; then re-enable collision detection on the element.
Post-place retreat: plan and execute a vertical retreat back to the original hover height using the same cost metric and collision-reduction settings.
Command Robot_1 to return to its predefined home configuration (move_to_home).

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Unique identifier of the 8 ft vertical element to be placed; must correspond to an existing USD prim under “/world/obstacles” and to the object currently attached to tool1 for detachment.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: Desired X coordinate of the element’s center in world space; used to compute robot approach positions.

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: Desired Y coordinate of the element’s center; also drives the lateral displacement of the Smart_Conveyor’s Joint_1 before placement.

arguments.name: Z
arguments.description: Desired Z coordinate of the element’s base; combined with the element width to derive vertical approach and placement heights.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Length of the element; half of this value is subtracted from the robot’s X approach position so the tool aligns with the element’s center.

arguments.name: W
arguments.description: Width (thickness) of the element; half of this value offsets the robot’s Z approach heights.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the element; not directly referenced inside the current procedure but retained for interface completeness and potential future use.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The procedure ends after the robot is back at its home pose with the element attached to the conveyor.
operational_notes: All motions are executed by Robot_1 using tcp “tool1”; no other robots are involved.
During placement the element’s gravity is momentarily disabled to prevent premature dropping; it is reattached to the conveyor with gravity turned off and collision subsequently re-enabled.
The function assumes the element is already grasped by Robot_1 via suction before invocation; no grasping action is commanded here.
The planner temporarily removes “/world/obstacles” and “Smart_Conveyor” primitives from collision consideration during the lowering and retreat motions to simplify path planning.
No conditional branches or retries are implemented; failure handling is not included.

operational_notes: All motions are executed by Robot_1 using tcp “tool1”; no other robots are involved.
During placement the element’s gravity is momentarily disabled to prevent premature dropping; it is reattached to the conveyor with gravity turned off and collision subsequently re-enabled.
The function assumes the element is already grasped by Robot_1 via suction before invocation; no grasping action is commanded here.
The planner temporarily removes “/world/obstacles” and “Smart_Conveyor” primitives from collision consideration during the lowering and retreat motions to simplify path planning.
No conditional branches or retries are implemented; failure handling is not included.

documentation.summary: Positions Robot_1’s suction end-effector above a specified 8-foot vertical element, lowers it to the final set-down height, detaches the element, mounts it onto the smart conveyor at the requested X-, Y-, Z-location, re-enables physics on the element, retracts the robot to a safe height, and finally returns the robot to its home pose.
procedure_steps: Translate Smart_Conveyor Joint_1 so that its pallet aligns with the requested Y coordinate; the joint offset is computed as −(Y − 1.8288)+2.275.
Declare a constant vertical Placement_Offset (0.00565 m) used to slightly shift the robot’s X approach positions.
Pre-place approach: plan a motion for Robot_1 with tcp “tool1” to a hover pose directly above the element’s target location (Z elevated by 1.02546 m plus half the element width). Execute the plan.

Place motion: plan and execute a second motion that lowers the tool by 0.10 m (from +1.02546 m to +0.92546 m above half width) to bring the element into contact with the conveyor. Path planning employs a grasp-approach cost metric along the Z axis and temporarily removes the obstacle and conveyor primitives from collision checking.
Command the end-effector to release the element (eef_detach) and disable gravity flag on the element so it can be kinematically attached to the moving conveyor.
Call Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv to rigidly attach the released element to the conveyor while keeping gravity disabled; then re-enable collision detection on the element.
Post-place retreat: plan and execute a vertical retreat back to the original hover height using the same cost metric and collision-reduction settings.
Command Robot_1 to return to its predefined home configuration (move_to_home).

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Unique identifier of the 8 ft vertical element to be placed; must correspond to an existing USD prim under “/world/obstacles” and to the object currently attached to tool1 for detachment.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: Desired X coordinate of the element’s center in world space; used to compute robot approach positions.

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: Desired Y coordinate of the element’s center; also drives the lateral displacement of the Smart_Conveyor’s Joint_1 before placement.

arguments.name: Z
arguments.description: Desired Z coordinate of the element’s base; combined with the element width to derive vertical approach and placement heights.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Length of the element; half of this value is subtracted from the robot’s X approach position so the tool aligns with the element’s center.

arguments.name: W
arguments.description: Width (thickness) of the element; half of this value offsets the robot’s Z approach heights.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the element; not directly referenced inside the current procedure but retained for interface completeness and potential future use.

documentation.returns: None. The procedure ends after the robot is back at its home pose with the element attached to the conveyor.

The function assumes the element is already grasped by Robot_1 via suction before invocation; no grasping action is commanded here.
The planner temporarily removes “/world/obstacles” and “Smart_Conveyor” primitives from collision consideration during the lowering and retreat motions to simplify path planning.
No conditional branches or retries are implemented; failure handling is not included.

```python
def Pick_2x10_Header(X: float = None,
        L: float = None,):
    global NUMBER_OF_HEADERS

    # Check if the Requested Length is less or equal to the Maximum Length of the Bear Loading elements pilled up
    if RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS[0] < L:
        print("The Requested Length is Less Than the Maximum Length of the Bear Loading Elements")
        return

    # Pick Orientation [0, ev, -ev, 0] => From The Pile
    # Pre Pick
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-1.306599901277,
                                  1.60499956533,
                                  0.484],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, -ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Pick
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-1.306599901277,
                                  1.60499956533,
                                  0.384],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, -ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    Robot_1.motion_gen_update_world()
    # "/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(it+1)
    Robot_1.eef_attach(tool_name="tool1", attaching_object_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))

    # Post Pick
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-1.306599901277,
                                  1.60499956533,
                                  0.484],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, -ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)
```

tool_arguments.summary: Moves Robot_1 (equipped with tool1) through a three-stage approach–grasp–retract sequence to pick a single 2 × 10 header from the Bear-Loading pile after confirming the requested stock length is available.
procedure_steps: Verify that the requested length L does not exceed RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS[0]; abort and log a message if it does.

Generate a ‘pre-pick’ motion plan that positions Robot_1’s tool1 100 mm above the pile at the fixed Cartesian pose [-1.306599901277, 1.60499956533, 0.484] and orientation [0, ev, -ev, 0]; execute the plan with visualisation disabled for collision spheres.

Generate a ‘pick’ motion plan that lowers the tool to z = 0.384 m (same x, y, orientation) while temporarily removing “Smart_Conveyor” and “world/obstacles” from the planner’s collision scene and biasing the planner with a grasp-approach metric along the z-axis; execute the plan.
Call motion_gen_update_world() so the planner’s world model reflects the robot’s new configuration.
Attach the picked work-piece named "Bear_Loading_Element_<NUMBER_OF_HEADERS>" to tool1 with eef_attach, registering the grasped object in the simulation.
Generate a ‘post-pick’ withdrawal plan that raises the tool back to the original pre-pick height (z = 0.484 m) using the same orientation and planning parameters; execute the plan.
Return with no explicit value.

arguments.description: Optional x-coordinate associated with the target header; currently not referenced inside the routine but retained for interface consistency.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Requested length of the header in simulation units; validated against RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS[0] to ensure the specified size is available before the pick is attempted.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function either exits early after printing a warning when the requested length is unavailable or completes the pick and returns implicitly with no value.

The orientation vector [0, ev, -ev, 0] is treated as a quaternion supplied by external context; its numerical values are not resolved within the function.
No loops, retries, or error recovery are implemented; motion and attachment commands are issued once per call.
Side selection or specific tool frames beyond tool1 are not handled; function is tailored for Robot_1 with the ‘tool1’ end-effector.
All actions are issued to a simulated environment; any physical execution depends on the underlying framework’s interpretation of plan(), render_exec(), motion_gen_update_world(), and eef_attach().

```python
def Cut_2x10_Header(L: float = None,
    W: float = None,
    H: float = None
):
    # Pre Cut
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-L + 1.60499956533,
                                  1.179599901277,
                                  W + 1.03],
                    target_orientation= [0, 0, -1, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Cut
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-L + 1.60499956533,
                                  1.179599901277,
                                  W + 0.53],
                    target_orientation= [0, 0, -1, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "World/obstacles/Small_Cutting_Table"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Simulated CUT
    Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool1", detaching_object_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))

    dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()
    test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS)).GetAttribute("physics:rigidBodyEnabled").Set(True)
    object = dc.get_rigid_body("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))
    object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)

    prims_utils.delete_prim("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))

    new_el = Cuboid(
        name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS),
        pose= [object_pose.p[0]-((RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS[0]-L)/2),
               object_pose.p[1],
               object_pose.p[2],
               object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
        dims= [H, L, W],
        color= [0.4, 0.2, 0, 1]
    )
    Add_Rigid_Object_To_Scene(test, "Cuboid", new_el)

    # Attaching the Cut Element
    Robot_1.eef_attach(tool_name="tool1", attaching_object_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))

    # Post Cut
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [-L + 1.60499956533,
                                  1.179599901277,
                                  W + 1.03],
                    target_orientation= [0, 0, -1, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "World/obstacles/Small_Cutting_Table"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)
```

tool_arguments.summary: Guides Robot_1, equipped with TCP "tool1", through a simulated lumber-cutting routine that replaces an oversize 2 × 10 header with a freshly sized cuboid matching the requested length (L), width (W) and height (H), then re-attaches the cut element for subsequent handling.
procedure_steps: 1. Pre-cut positioning – plans a motion that drives Robot_1’s TCP (tool1) to a safe hover pose located at X = –L + 1.60499956533, Y = 1.179599901277, Z = W + 1.03 with a fixed orientation [0, 0, –1, 0]; the plan is executed immediately via render_exec, updating the world model.

2. Cutting approach – generates a second plan that lowers the TCP to the cut level (Z = W + 0.53) while instructing the planner to ignore the "Smart_Conveyor", "/world/obstacles" and the small cutting table and to bias the path with a grasp-approach cost metric along the tool’s Z-axis; the motion is executed.
3. Simulated saw activation – detaches the currently grasped object "Bear_Loading_Element_<NUMBER_OF_HEADERS>" from the end effector to represent releasing the board for cutting.
4. Physics activation – acquires the simulation’s dynamic-control interface, enables rigid-body physics on the released element, and records its 6-DOF pose.

5. Material replacement – deletes the original oversized primitive and instantiates a new Cuboid named "Bear_Loading_Element_<NUMBER_OF_HEADERS>" whose pose is shifted in X by –(RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS[0] – L)/2 to represent the trimmed length and whose dimensions are set to [H, L, W]; the new rigid body is then added to the scene.
6. Re-attachment – attaches the newly created, correctly sized header back to tool1.

7. Post-cut retreat – plans and executes a motion that returns the TCP to the original pre-cut hover pose, again using the obstacle-filtered, grasp-approach biased planner settings, and renders the execution.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Desired finished length of the 2 × 10 header in simulation length units.

arguments.name: W
arguments.description: Width (thickness) of the header in simulation units; also used to compute safe Z offsets when positioning the tool above and at the cut plane.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the header (2-inch nominal side) in simulation units; supplied to size the new cuboid created after the cut.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function completes after the robot has repositioned with the newly created, length-adjusted header attached.
operational_notes: Relies on global symbols NUMBER_OF_HEADERS, RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS, test, prims_utils and _dynamic_control that must exist in the caller’s scope.

The physical separation of the board is not modeled as a material removal process; instead, the function emulates cutting by deleting the original prim and spawning a shorter cuboid with updated dimensions.
During cutting approach and retreat, specific environment primitives are excluded from collision checking to prevent planning failures.
All motions are executed with Robot_1’s TCP named "tool1"; no alternative side or robot selection exists in this routine.
Function assumes that input dimensions are valid and reachable; it does not perform argument validation or error handling.

arguments.description: Height of the header (2-inch nominal side) in simulation units; supplied to size the new cuboid created after the cut.

documentation.returns: None. The function completes after the robot has repositioned with the newly created, length-adjusted header attached.
operational_notes: Relies on global symbols NUMBER_OF_HEADERS, RAW_HEADER_DIMENSIONS, test, prims_utils and _dynamic_control that must exist in the caller’s scope.

The physical separation of the board is not modeled as a material removal process; instead, the function emulates cutting by deleting the original prim and spawning a shorter cuboid with updated dimensions.
During cutting approach and retreat, specific environment primitives are excluded from collision checking to prevent planning failures.
All motions are executed with Robot_1’s TCP named "tool1"; no alternative side or robot selection exists in this routine.
Function assumes that input dimensions are valid and reachable; it does not perform argument validation or error handling.

```python
def Place_2x10_Header(X: float = None,
    Y: float = None,
    Z: float = None,
    L: float = None,
    W: float = None,
    H: float = None
):
    global NUMBER_OF_HEADERS

    # Moving Conveyor
    Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', -(Y - 1.8288) - L / 2 + 2.87999956533)

    Conv_Curr_Loc: float = -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275

    # Pre Place
    Place_Doable: bool = Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [X + 1.312189901277,
                                  0,
                                  W + 1.22546],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)

    if Place_Doable == True:
        # Execute Pre Drop
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)
        # Drop
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [X + 1.312189901277,
                                    0,
                                    W + 1.07546],
                        target_orientation= [0, ev, ev, 0],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool1", detaching_object_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS)).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(False)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))
        # Enabling Colliders
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS)).GetAttribute("physics:collisionEnabled").Set(True)
    else:
        # Automatic Placement of the Bear Loading when Robot 1 Can't Place it
        # Pass To Human Location
        Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [X - W / 2 + 1.10259,
                                      0,
                                      H + 1.254659802554],
                        target_orientation= [0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool1", detaching_object_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))

        # [ev, 0, ev, 0]
        dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()
        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS)).GetAttribute("physics:rigidBodyEnabled").Set(True)
        object = dc.get_rigid_body("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS))
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)

        # Create new position
        New_Loc = Gf.Vec3d(object_pose.p[0], object_pose.p[1], Z + 0.89546)
        # Create a valid quaternion and rotation
        quat = Gf.Quatd(1, 0, 0, 0)  # Ensure this follows (real, x, y, z)
        New_Or = Gf.Rotation(quat)
        # Convert quaternion to required format
        quat_as_tuple = (New_Or.GetQuat().GetReal(), *New_Or.GetQuat().GetImaginary())
        # Create a Transform object
        new_transform = _dynamic_control.Transform()
        new_transform.p.x = New_Loc[0]
        new_transform.p.y = New_Loc[1]
        new_transform.p.z = New_Loc[2]
        new_transform.r.w = quat_as_tuple[0]  # real part
        new_transform.r.x = quat_as_tuple[1]
        new_transform.r.y = quat_as_tuple[2]
        new_transform.r.z = quat_as_tuple[3]
        # Apply transformation
        dc.set_rigid_body_pose(object, new_transform)

        test._stage.GetPrimAtPath("/world/obstacles/Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS)).GetAttribute("physxRigidBody:disableGravity").Set(True)
        Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name= "Bear_Loading_Element_"+str(NUMBER_OF_HEADERS), Enable_Gravity= False)

    Robot_1.move_to_home()

    # We Used The Top Bear Loading Element
    NUMBER_OF_HEADERS-=1

    return Conv_Curr_Loc
```

tool_arguments.summary: Positions a gripped “Bear Loading” 2 × 10 header on a moving Smart_Conv conveyor with Robot_1’s TCP "tool1".  The routine first aligns the conveyor, then tries to place the header directly at the requested X-, Y-, Z-derived location.  If the robot’s motion planner reports that the direct pose is unreachable, the header is instead dropped at an accessible human-handoff location and, through the dynamic-control interface, is kinematically repositioned onto the conveyor with gravity disabled.  In either case the header is detached from the tool, optionally has gravity re-enabled, is welded to the conveyor, and Robot_1 returns home before the global header counter is decremented.
procedure_steps: Command Smart_Conv joint "Joint_1" to translate by a distance equal to -(Y – 1.8288) – L⁄2 + 2.87999956533 so the conveyor lines up with the desired placement Y coordinate.
Compute and store the conveyor’s current logical location as Conv_Curr_Loc = -(Y – 1.8288) + 2.275 for later return.
Ask Robot_1’s motion planner, using TCP "tool1", to reach a pre-drop pose located at [X + 1.312189901277, 0, W + 1.22546] with quaternion [0, ev, ev, 0]; the boolean result is saved as Place_Doable.

If Place_Doable is True (direct placement is reachable):
 a. Execute the pre-drop trajectory (render_exec).
 b. Plan and execute a second, lower "drop" pose at [X + 1.312189901277, 0, W + 1.07546] with the same orientation.
 c. Detach the header from "tool1" (object name "Bear_Loading_Element_<NUMBER_OF_HEADERS>").
 d. Re-enable gravity on the header and attach it to the conveyor via Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv.
 e. Re-enable physics collision on the header.

Else (direct placement not feasible):
 a. Plan and execute a hand-off motion to [X – W⁄2 + 1.10259, 0, H + 1.254659802554] with quaternion [0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5].
 b. Detach the header from "tool1".
 c. Through the dynamic-control interface, enable the header’s rigid-body simulation, read its current pose, construct a new pose whose Z component is raised to Z + 0.89546, and apply this transform to the rigid body.
 d. Disable gravity on the header and attach it to the conveyor with gravity left disabled.
Command Robot_1 to move to its predefined home position.
Decrement the global NUMBER_OF_HEADERS counter to reflect that the top header has been used.
Return the computed Conv_Curr_Loc value so downstream routines know the conveyor’s updated position.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: Target X coordinate around which the header should be placed or handed off, expressed in simulator length units.

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: Target Y coordinate of the desired conveyor placement location, also used to determine conveyor travel offset.

arguments.name: Z
arguments.description: Reference Z coordinate used when repositioning the header during the fallback (human hand-off) branch.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Length of the 2 × 10 header; used to fine-tune conveyor alignment distance.

arguments.name: W
arguments.description: Width of the header; used to compute both pre-drop and drop heights above the conveyor.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Height of the header; used to define the fallback hand-off pose when direct placement is impossible.

tool_arguments.returns: Float value Conv_Curr_Loc: the conveyor’s longitudinal position after motion, enabling subsequent tasks to know where the header was placed.
operational_notes: Function depends on global NUMBER_OF_HEADERS to name the header instance and to track remaining stock; it is decremented after each successful call.
Branching on Place_Doable allows automatic recovery when the robot cannot reach the target pose; fallback uses dynamic control rather than kinematic execution.
Gravity and collision flags on the header are toggled differently depending on whether the header is placed directly (gravity enabled) or repositioned programmatically (gravity disabled).
Orientation arrays reference an external variable ‘ev’ whose value is assumed to be defined elsewhere in the environment.
Robot_1 always returns to its home pose, ensuring predictable starting conditions for subsequent operations.
