```python
def Nail_2x10_Header(push_to_nail: float = 0.025,
                        el_pose: List[float] = [],
                        el_dims: List[float] = [],
                        conv_current_location: float = None):
    quat = R.from_euler('xyz', [((np.pi/2)+np.radians(BR_NAILING_ANGLE))*(-1), 0, (np.pi/2)]).as_quat()
    quat[1] *= -1

    # Left Nail
    if(conv_current_location+(el_dims[0]/2)+NAILING_CONV_TARGET > SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1):
        print("Left Nail is Not Possible ! Due To Conveyor Reachability")
    else:
        Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', conv_current_location + el_dims[0] / 2 + 0.5)
        # Robot 1 Move For Nail
        # Pre Nail
        Nail_Doable: bool = Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                        target_pose= [el_pose[0] + 0.2 * el_dims[2] + 1.13259,
                                    (el_dims[1] / 2 + 0.070710678119) * -1 + 0.5,
                                    el_pose[2] + 0.986490678119],
                        target_orientation= [quat[3], quat[0], quat[1], quat[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        if (Nail_Doable == True):
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()

            # Nail 1
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)


            # Nail 1 Backward
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)
            
            # Nail 2 Prep
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0] - 0.4 * el_dims[2],
                                        object_pose.p[1],
                                        object_pose.p[2]],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=1))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)
            
            # Nail 2
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 2 Backward
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.866025403785,
                                        object_pose.p[2] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.866025403785) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.5],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            Robot_1.move_to_home(removing_primitives= ["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"])
        else:
            print("Left Nail is Not Possible ! Due To Robot NailGun Reachability")

    #NAILING OTHER SIDE
    quat = R.from_euler('xyz', [((np.pi/2)+np.radians(BR_NAILING_ANGLE)), 0, (np.pi/2)*(-1)]).as_quat()
    quat[1] *= -1

    # Right Nail
    if(conv_current_location-(el_dims[0]/2)-NAILING_CONV_TARGET < 0):
        print("Right Nail is Not Possible ! Due To Conveyor Reachability")
    else:
        Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', conv_current_location - el_dims[0] / 2 - 0.5)

        # Pre Nail
        Nail_Doable: bool = Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                        target_pose= [el_pose[0] + 0.2 * el_dims[2] + 1.13259,
                                    el_dims[1] / 2 - 0.429289321881,
                                    el_pose[2] + 0.986490678119],
                        target_orientation= [quat[3], quat[0], quat[1], quat[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True)

        if(Nail_Doable == True):

            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()

            # Nail 1
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] + -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 1 Backward
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] - -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 2 Prep
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0] - 0.4 * el_dims[2],
                                        object_pose.p[1],
                                        object_pose.p[2]],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=1))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)
    
            # Nail 2
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] + -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] - (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            # Nail 2 Backward
            object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
            object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
            Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                            target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                        object_pose.p[1] - -1 * (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781187,
                                        object_pose.p[2] + (-(el_dims[1] / 2 / 0.707106781187) + push_to_nail + 0.1) * 0.707106781186],
                            target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                            update_world_needed= True,
                            removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                            direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
            Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                    Show_Sphere= False)

            Robot_1.move_to_home(removing_primitives= ["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"])
        else:
            print("Right Nail is Not Possible ! Due To Robot NailGun Reachability")
```

tool_arguments.summary: Moves the Smart Conveyor and commands Robot_1 – equipped with TCP "tool2" nail-gun – to insert two nails into each end of a 2 × 10 header lumber element. The routine first nails the left end, then the right end, provided both the conveyor and the robot can reach the required poses. All motions are planned, rendered, and executed through the robot-controller interfaces; each nail is driven by a short linear push along the tool axis followed by a retreat.
procedure_steps: Derive a left-side nailing orientation quaternion from BR_NAILING_ANGLE by composing two rotations and inverting the Y component to align the nail-gun with the desired 45 ° downward trajectory.

Left-end feasibility check: verify that translating Smart Conveyor joint 1 to conv_current_location + el_dims[0]/2 + NAILING_CONV_TARGET is still within SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1. If not, abort left-side nailing and issue a console warning.
If reachable, move the conveyor by calling Smart_Conv.render_exec to place the header’s left end 0.5 m in front of Robot_1.

Plan a pre-nailing approach for Robot_1 (TCP "tool2") to a pose 0.2·el_dims[2] ahead of the header’s near face and 0.5 m above the conveyor, using the previously computed orientation. If planning fails, report robot reachability failure and skip left-side nailing.
If planning succeeds, execute the pre-nail motion with Robot_1.render_exec and connect to the low-level dynamic control interface (_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface).

For the first left-side nail: retrieve the current tool pose, plan a linear push along the tool’s approach axis by (push_to_nail + 0.1 m) at 45 ° downward, excluding the Smart Conveyor and world obstacles from collision checking; execute the plan.
Plan and execute a symmetric retreat to withdraw the nail-gun from the timber.
Translate the TCP –0.4·el_dims[2] along the board length to the second left-side nailing location; execute the move.
Repeat the push-in and retreat sequence for the second left-side nail.
Return Robot_1 to its home position while temporarily removing conveyor and obstacle primitives from the planning scene.
Compute a mirrored orientation quaternion for right-side nailing (rotation about +X and –Z).
Right-end feasibility check: ensure the conveyor can reach conv_current_location – el_dims[0]/2 – NAILING_CONV_TARGET ≥ 0; otherwise print a reachability warning and abort right-side nailing.

If reachable, move the conveyor so the header’s right end is 0.5 m in front of the robot and repeat the same planning/execution sequence (pre-nail approach, first nail push/retreat, translation, second nail push/retreat, return home) mirrored about the Y axis.

arguments.name: push_to_nail
arguments.description: Additional linear distance (in metres) that the nail-gun TCP advances into the timber during each nailing push; defaults to 0.025 m.

arguments.name: el_pose
arguments.description: List of three floats giving the XYZ origin of the header element in robot base coordinates; indices 0 and 2 (X and Z) are used to compute approach positions.

arguments.name: el_dims
arguments.description: List of three floats representing the header’s length (index 0), height/thickness (index 1), and width (index 2); required for conveyor offsets and inter-nail spacing.

arguments.name: conv_current_location
arguments.description: Current position (in metres or joint units consistent with Smart Conveyor calibration) of Smart Conveyor joint 1; used to check reachability and command new conveyor targets.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function terminates silently when all commanded motions have been rendered/executed, or prints diagnostic messages if either side of the header cannot be reached.
operational_notes: Relies on global constants BR_NAILING_ANGLE, NAILING_CONV_TARGET, SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1, and on globally available objects Robot_1, Smart_Conv, PoseCostMetric, and _dynamic_control.
All robot motions are generated through Robot_1.plan and executed via Robot_1.render_exec; no actual tool-trigger signal is issued—only positional pushes and retreats are performed.
Collision checking excludes the Smart Conveyor and world obstacles during nailing motions by specifying removing_primitives.
If either the conveyor or the robot cannot reach a requested pose, the corresponding side is skipped after printing a warning; the other side may still be processed.
The function assumes el_pose and el_dims contain at least three numerical elements; missing or malformed data will raise index errors at runtime.
After completing each side, the robot is returned to its predefined home position to clear the workspace.

```python
def Complementary_Nail_Operation(push_to_nail: float = 0.05,
                         H: float = None):
    for target in Smart_Conv._nail_poses:
        # Check if the target is reachable or not (for the conveyor !)
        if(target[0]+(NAILING_CONV_TARGET*target[1]) > SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1):
            Replacing_Target: Tuple[float, float] = (target[0] - 1.0, -target[1])
            target = Replacing_Target

        Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', target[0] + 0.5 * target[1])
        # Robot 1 Nail
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [3.69, 0.5 * target[1] * 2, H - 0.7 * H + 0.89546],
                        target_orientation= [0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5],
                        update_world_needed= True)
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()
        object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool1")

        # Restricted Bot Nail
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
        Robot_2.release_path_plan_restriction()
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [object_pose.p[0] - push_to_nail, object_pose.p[1], object_pose.p[2]],
                        target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)
        # Nail Done
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
        Robot_2.release_path_plan_restriction()
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [object_pose.p[0] + push_to_nail, object_pose.p[1], object_pose.p[2]],
                        target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)
        
        # Other Nail
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
        Robot_2.release_path_plan_restriction()
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [object_pose.p[0], object_pose.p[1], object_pose.p[2] + 0.4 * H],
                        target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=1))
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

        # Restricted Top Nail
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
        Robot_2.release_path_plan_restriction()
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [object_pose.p[0] - push_to_nail, object_pose.p[1], object_pose.p[2]],
                        target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)
        # Restricted Top Nail
        object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
        Robot_2.release_path_plan_restriction()
        Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                        target_pose= [object_pose.p[0] + push_to_nail, object_pose.p[1], object_pose.p[2]],
                        target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                        update_world_needed= True,
                        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                        direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
        Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                Show_Sphere= False)

    Robot_2.move_to_home()
```

tool_arguments.summary: Coordinates the conveyor and Robot_2 (equipped with TCP "tool1") so the robot drives a nail at two vertical levels for every pose stored in Smart_Conv._nail_poses. The routine advances the conveyor to each nail-pose, positions the robot in front of the workpiece, performs a forward push to seat the nail, retracts, elevates to the higher nail location, and repeats the push–retract cycle before returning the robot to its home pose.
procedure_steps: Iterate over every (joint_offset , side_flag) tuple contained in Smart_Conv._nail_poses.

For each target, verify that commanding the conveyor’s Joint_1 to joint_offset + NAILING_CONV_TARGET*side_flag would remain inside SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1; if not, replace the target with (joint_offset – 1.0, –side_flag) so the motion stays reachable.
Command the Smart_Conv conveyor to rotate Joint_1 to the corrected value joint_offset + 0.5*side_flag, visually rendering the execution.

Plan and execute with Robot_2 (TCP "tool1") a Cartesian move that brings the nail-gun in front of the lower nail position at coordinates [3.69, side_flag, 0.3*H + 0.89546] with a fixed quaternion [0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5].
Acquire the dynamic-control interface, obtain the rigid-body handle for the robot’s tool, and read its current pose (object_pose).

---------- Lower nail insertion cycle ----------
 a. Clear any path-planning restrictions.
 b. Plan and execute a linear push along the tool’s –X direction by "push_to_nail" (object_pose.p[0] – push_to_nail) using a grasp-approach cost metric that biases motion along the tool’s Z axis; execute the plan.
 c. Re-acquire the tool pose, clear restrictions, and plan a symmetric retraction by the same offset in +X (object_pose.p[0] + push_to_nail); execute.

---------- Transition to upper nail position ----------
 a. Re-acquire the tool pose.
 b. Clear restrictions and plan a vertical move that raises the tool by 0.4*H along +Z; execute.

---------- Upper nail insertion cycle ----------
 a. Re-acquire the tool pose, clear restrictions, and plan a second forward push by "push_to_nail" along –X; execute.
 b. Re-acquire the tool pose, clear restrictions, and plan the final retraction by the same amount along +X; execute.
After all target nail poses have been serviced, command Robot_2 to move to its predefined home configuration.

arguments.name: push_to_nail
arguments.description: Linear offset (in metres) by which the robot advances and then retracts the nail-gun TCP along its local –X/+X axis during each nailing action. Defaults to 0.05.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Nominal height of the workpiece or element, used to compute both the initial approach height and the vertical offset to the second (upper) nail position. Must be provided; units match the simulation’s length units.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The function ends after completing all planned and executed motions without explicitly returning a value.
operational_notes: The routine relies on global constants NAILING_CONV_TARGET and SMART_CONV_RANGE_OF_MOTION_J1 to ensure conveyor reachability.
Path-planning obstacles "Smart_Conveyor" and "world/obstacles" are temporarily removed whenever direct linear pushes or retractions are planned.
PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric is configured to favour motion predominantly along a single axis (axis 2 for push/retract, axis 1 for vertical move).
All executions are rendered for visualization via Smart_Conv.render_exec and Robot_2.render_exec; no physical nail-firing command is issued.
Function assumes H is not None even though it defaults to None; passing None will raise a TypeError during arithmetic.

```python
def Pick_OSB_Plate(el_name: str = None,
        L: float = None,
        W: float = None,
        H: float = None):
    # Pre Pick
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [0.179599901277,
                                  -2.08499956533,
                                  H + 0.7],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True)
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)

    # Pick
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [0.179599901277,
                                  -2.08499956533,
                                  H + 0.63],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)
    
    Create_Wooden_Element_For_Sheathing_Table(el_name= el_name, L= L, W= W, H= H)

    # Attach
    Robot_1.eef_attach(tool_name= "tool1", attaching_object_name= el_name)

    # Post Pick
    Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool1",
                    target_pose= [0.179599901277,
                                  -2.08499956533,
                                  H + 0.7],
                    target_orientation= [0, ev, ev, 0],
                    update_world_needed= True,
                    removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles", "World/obstacles/Sheathing_Table"],
                    direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                            Show_Sphere= False)
```

tool_arguments.summary: Plans and executes Robot_1 motions that approach, grasp and lift an oriented-strand-board (OSB) plate from the sheathing table, adds the newly created plate object to the simulation scene, and attaches it to the robot’s TCP "tool1" for subsequent transport.
procedure_steps: Compute and execute a pre-pick approach: Robot_1 plans a Cartesian motion for TCP "tool1" to a position directly above the plate at X 0.1796 m, Y –2.0850 m and Z set to H+0.70 m, using the supplied orientation vector [0, ev, ev, 0]; the plan is rendered and executed.

Plan and execute the descent to the grasp height: the robot lowers the TCP to Z = H+0.63 m at the same X-Y coordinates, requesting the planner to ignore the named primitives ("Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles") and to bias the path with a grasp-approach cost metric that constrains motion mainly along the Z-axis (linear_axis=2); the trajectory is then rendered and executed.
Instantiate the OSB plate in the virtual workcell by calling Create_Wooden_Element_For_Sheathing_Table with the given geometric dimensions and element name.
Physically attach the newly created plate to Robot_1’s end effector by invoking eef_attach on "tool1" with the provided element name, establishing a rigid grasp in the simulation.

Plan and execute the withdrawal move: the robot retracts the TCP back to the original approach height (Z = H+0.70 m) while additionally requesting the planner to disregard the sheathing table obstacle ("World/obstacles/Sheathing_Table"); the same grasp-approach cost metric is applied and the motion is rendered and executed.

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Unique identifier assigned to the OSB plate object; used both for spawning the plate in the simulation and for attaching it to the robot’s end effector.

arguments.name: L
arguments.description: Longitudinal length of the OSB plate, forwarded to the element-creation routine.

arguments.name: W
arguments.description: Width of the OSB plate, forwarded to the element-creation routine.

arguments.name: H
arguments.description: Thickness (height) of the OSB plate; also serves as a reference offset for computing the robot’s Z-level approach and grasp poses (H+0.70 m and H+0.63 m).

tool_arguments.returns: None – the procedure performs motion execution, object creation, and attachment side-effects without returning a value.
operational_notes: All motions are executed sequentially; failure handling is not implemented in this routine.
The planner is instructed to remove specified environment primitives during the descent and retraction phases to avoid collision checks with the conveyor, generic obstacles, and the sheathing table.
The direct_pose_cost parameter enforces a straight-line approach and retreat predominantly along the Z-axis to maintain a safe withdrawal path.
Successful execution assumes that the variable ‘ev’ contains a valid orientation component and that the supplied height H allows the computed Z targets to be reachable.

```python
def Place_OSB_Plate(el_name: str = None,
        X: float = None,
        Y: float = None,
        Z: float = None) -> float:
    # Letting Space For the First Robot To Place The Sheathing Plate
    Robot_2.move_to_home(Customized_JS=[np.radians(90), -0.5, 0.5, 0, 0, 0])

    # Moving the Conveyor
    Smart_Conv.render_exec(
        'Joint_1',
        -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.095400098723
    )

    # Pre Place
    Robot_1.plan(
        tcp_name="tool1",
        target_pose=[
            -X + 3.17678956533,
            0,
            Z + 0.99546,
        ],
        target_orientation=[0, 0, -1, 0],
        update_world_needed=True,
    )
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance=True, Show_Sphere=False)

    # Place
    Robot_1.plan(
        tcp_name="tool1",
        target_pose=[
            -X + 3.27678956533,
            0,
            Z + 0.92546,
        ],
        target_orientation=[0, 0, -1, 0],
        update_world_needed=True,
        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
        orientational_restriction=torch.tensor([1, 1, 1], dtype=torch.float32),
    )
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance=True, Show_Sphere=False)

    # Detach
    Robot_1.eef_detach(tool_name="tool1", detaching_object_name=el_name)
    Smart_Conv.attach_object_to_conv(obj_name=el_name, Enable_Gravity=False)

    # Post Place
    Robot_1.plan(
        tcp_name="tool1",
        target_pose=[
            -X + 3.17678956533,
            0,
            Z + 0.99546,
        ],
        target_orientation=[0, 0, -1, 0],
        update_world_needed=True,
        removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
        orientational_restriction=torch.tensor([1, 1, 1], dtype=torch.float32),
    )
    Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance=True, Show_Sphere=False)

    # Move To Home
    Robot_1.move_to_home()
    Robot_2.move_to_home()

    # Compute and return conveyor location
    Sht_plate_on_conv_loc: float = (
        -(Y - 1.8288) + 2.275
    )
    return Sht_plate_on_conv_loc
```

tool_arguments.summary: Coordinates two robots and a smart conveyor to deposit an oriented OSB sheathing plate at a Cartesian target and leave it fixed on the conveyor, finally reporting the conveyor’s linear position that now corresponds to the plate location.
procedure_steps: Command Robot_2 to leave the shared workspace by moving to a custom joint-space home pose so Robot_1 can work unobstructed.
Translate the Smart_Conv conveyor’s primary axis joint ("Joint_1") by the offset -(Y − 1.8288) + 2.095400098723 to bring the conveyor surface under the desired Y coordinate.
Plan and execute a "pre-place" Cartesian approach with Robot_1 using TCP "tool1" to a pose directly above the target location (x = −X + 3.17678956533, z = Z + 0.99546).

Plan and execute the final "place" motion: Robot_1 lowers and advances to pose (x = −X + 3.27678956533, z = Z + 0.92546) while maintaining orientation [0,0,−1,0], updating the world model, ignoring the Smart_Conveyor and generic obstacle primitives, and enforcing full orientational restriction.
Detach the gripped OSB plate from Robot_1’s end effector (tool1) and immediately attach the same object to the conveyor with gravity disabled so it remains fixed in place.
Plan and execute a retreat: Robot_1 lifts back to the earlier pre-place pose, again omitting the conveyor and obstacle primitives from collision consideration.
Return both robots to their nominal home configurations (Robot_1 default home, Robot_2 custom home previously defined).
Compute the resulting conveyor joint position that aligns with the deposited plate as Sht_plate_on_conv_loc =  -(Y − 1.8288) + 2.275 and return this value.

arguments.name: el_name
arguments.description: Unique identifier of the OSB plate object that is detached from Robot_1’s tool and attached to the conveyor during placement.

arguments.name: X
arguments.description: Target X coordinate of the plate in world space; used to compute the robot’s approach and placement poses (negated and offset inside the planner).

arguments.name: Y
arguments.description: Target Y coordinate of the plate in world space; drives the conveyor translation and is reused to calculate the returned conveyor location.

arguments.name: Z
arguments.description: Target Z coordinate of the plate in world space; determines the vertical level for the robot’s pre-place and place poses.

tool_arguments.returns: A float holding Sht_plate_on_conv_loc, the final commanded position for the conveyor’s Joint_1 after placement, corresponding to the plate’s location along the conveyor.
operational_notes: Relies on Robot_1 and Robot_2 instances and a Smart_Conv conveyor object being available in the execution context.
Orientation is fixed to a downward-facing quaternion [0,0,−1,0]; orientational_restriction is set to [1,1,1] to enforce full orientation adherence during motion planning.
Gravity is explicitly disabled for the plate after attachment to the conveyor, so the physical simulation must support gravity toggling for individual objects.
No conditional branches or iterative loops are present; the sequence executes deterministically as listed.

```python
def Nail_OSB_Plate(push_to_nail: float = 0.01,
                     nail_num: int = 6,
                     el_dims: list[float] = [],
                     Sht_plate_on_conv_loc: float = None):
    for nail in Smart_Conv._vertical_nail_poses:
        # It means that we should vertically nail the sheathing plate to the king at these locations
        if(nail <= Sht_plate_on_conv_loc + (el_dims[1]/2) and nail >= Sht_plate_on_conv_loc - (el_dims[1]/2)):
            # Move Nail Target To Y=0
            Smart_Conv.render_exec('Joint_1', nail)

            Starting_X: float = 1.15259
            Ending_X: float = 3.63099
            # Might Be Less Than 1, so 2 decimals
            L: float = np.round((Ending_X - Starting_X) / (nail_num - 1.0), 2)
            it: int = 0
            dc=_dynamic_control.acquire_dynamic_control_interface()

            # Robot 1 vertical nailing
            while it < (nail_num/2):
                # Pre Nail Rob1
                Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool3",
                                target_pose= [it * L + 1.15259,
                                            0,
                                            el_dims[2] + 1.04706],
                                target_orientation= [1, 0, 0, 0],
                                update_world_needed= True)
                Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                        Show_Sphere= False)

                # Nail Rob1
                object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool3")
                object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
                Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool3",
                                target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                            object_pose.p[1],
                                            object_pose.p[2] - push_to_nail - 0.05],
                                target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                                update_world_needed= True,
                                removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                                direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
                Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                        Show_Sphere= False)

                # Post Nail Rob1
                object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_1._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool3")
                object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
                Robot_1.plan(tcp_name= "tool3",
                                target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                            object_pose.p[1],
                                            object_pose.p[2] + push_to_nail + 0.05],
                                target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                                update_world_needed= True,
                                removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                                direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
                Robot_1.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                        Show_Sphere= False)
                it+=1

            # Reset iterator and send Robot 1 home
            it: int = 0
            Robot_1.move_to_home()

            # Robot 2 vertical nailing
            while it < (nail_num/2):
                # Pre Nail Rob2
                Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                                target_pose= [-(it * L) + 3.63099,
                                            0,
                                            el_dims[2] + 1.04706],
                                target_orientation= [1, 0, 0, 0],
                                update_world_needed= True)
                Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                        Show_Sphere= False)

                # Nail Rob2
                object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
                object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
                Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                                target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                            object_pose.p[1],
                                            object_pose.p[2] - push_to_nail - 0.05],
                                target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                                update_world_needed= True,
                                removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                                direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
                Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                        Show_Sphere= False)

                # Post Nail Rob2
                object=dc.get_rigid_body("/"+Robot_2._ROS_JS_robot_indicator+"/tool2")
                object_pose=dc.get_rigid_body_pose(object)
                Robot_2.plan(tcp_name= "tool2",
                                target_pose= [object_pose.p[0],
                                            object_pose.p[1],
                                            object_pose.p[2] + push_to_nail + 0.05],
                                target_orientation= [object_pose.r[3], object_pose.r[0], object_pose.r[1], object_pose.r[2]],
                                update_world_needed= True,
                                removing_primitives=["Smart_Conveyor", "world/obstacles"],
                                direct_pose_cost= PoseCostMetric.create_grasp_approach_metric(offset_position=0.0, tstep_fraction=0.001,linear_axis=2))
                Robot_2.render_exec(renderInstance= True,
                                        Show_Sphere= False)
                it+=1

            Robot_2.move_to_home()
```

tool_arguments.summary: Coordinates Robot_1 (tool3) and Robot_2 (tool2) together with the Smart_Conv conveyor to execute a programmed sequence of vertical nailing motions that fasten an oriented-strand-board (OSB) sheathing plate to a framing member.  For every candidate nail location that lies beneath the plate, the routine re-positions the conveyor, then iteratively plans and renders approach, push-in, and retract trajectories for each robot so that an even number of nails are driven across the plate width.
procedure_steps: Iterate over every linear position value stored in Smart_Conv._vertical_nail_poses.
For each candidate position, check whether it resides inside the current sheathing-plate window defined by Sht_plate_on_conv_loc ± (el_dims[1] / 2).  Skip positions that are outside this range.
For every accepted position:
a. Command the Smart_Conv conveyor’s axis ‘Joint_1’ to move to the selected nail Y-coordinate by calling Smart_Conv.render_exec.
b. Declare the fixed X-axis span of the plate (Starting_X = 1.15259 m, Ending_X = 3.63099 m) and compute the uniform nail spacing L = round((Ending_X – Starting_X)/(nail_num – 1), 2).
c. Obtain a dynamic-control interface handle (dc = acquire_dynamic_control_interface()) for direct access to the real-time robot state models.
d. Vertical nailing with Robot_1 (tool3) for the first half of the requested nails:
i.  Loop while the iterator ‘it’ is less than nail_num/2:
• Plan and render a pre-nail approach that positions tool3 at [Starting_X + it × L, 0, el_dims[2] + 1.04706] with orientation [1, 0, 0, 0] and update the world state.

• Retrieve the current rigid-body pose of tool3 via dc, then plan a linear push-in along the tool Z-axis to [same X,Y, Z – (push_to_nail + 0.05)] using a grasp-approach cost metric and excluding the conveyor and obstacle geometry; render the execution.
• Query the refreshed tool pose and plan a retract motion back to [same X,Y, Z + (push_to_nail + 0.05)] with identical orientation and cost constraints; render the execution.
• Increment the iterator.
e. Command Robot_1 to return to its predefined home position.
f. Reset the iterator and repeat an analogous loop with Robot_2 (tool2) for the remaining half of the nails:
i.  For each index, plan and render a pre-nail approach to [Ending_X – it × L, 0, el_dims[2] + 1.04706] with orientation [1, 0, 0, 0].

ii. Execute the push-in and retract pair of linear motions identical in magnitude and cost settings to those used by Robot_1, again excluding the conveyor and global obstacles from collision checking.
iii. Increment the iterator until all remaining nails are processed.
g. After finishing, send Robot_2 back to its home configuration.
Exit the function implicitly without returning a value.

arguments.name: push_to_nail
arguments.description: Additional linear penetration depth (in metres) applied along the tool Z-axis during each nail push stroke; the robot descends this distance plus 0.05 m before retracting by the same amount.

arguments.name: nail_num
arguments.description: Total number of nails to place across the plate width; assumed even so it can be split equally between the two robots.

arguments.name: el_dims
arguments.description: List containing the sheathing plate’s dimensions; element [1] (height) bounds the acceptable Y-range for nailing, and element [2] (thickness) is used to set the initial approach height.

arguments.name: Sht_plate_on_conv_loc
arguments.description: Current Y-axis location of the sheathing plate’s centre on the conveyor; used to match candidate nail positions to the actual plate position.

tool_arguments.returns: None. The procedure completes after all eligible nail locations have been serviced and both robots return to their home poses.
operational_notes: The function assumes ‘nail_num’ is an even integer; each robot handles exactly half of the nails.
No explicit command to trigger the nail gun hardware is issued; the robots only perform motion plans that position the tool tips for a presumed external nailing action.
The conveyor is repositioned to align the target nail Y-coordinate under the robot workspace before any robotic motion begins.
Each push-in includes a fixed 0.05 m bias in addition to the user-supplied ‘push_to_nail’ depth to guarantee surface contact.
During push and retract phases, collision checking excludes ‘Smart_Conveyor’ and ‘world/obstacles’ primitives, relying on PoseCostMetric for linear Z-axis constraints.
Robots return to their predefined home configurations after finishing their respective nailing sequences.

documentation.returns: None. The procedure completes after all eligible nail locations have been serviced and both robots return to their home poses.
operational_notes: The function assumes ‘nail_num’ is an even integer; each robot handles exactly half of the nails.
No explicit command to trigger the nail gun hardware is issued; the robots only perform motion plans that position the tool tips for a presumed external nailing action.
The conveyor is repositioned to align the target nail Y-coordinate under the robot workspace before any robotic motion begins.
Each push-in includes a fixed 0.05 m bias in addition to the user-supplied ‘push_to_nail’ depth to guarantee surface contact.
During push and retract phases, collision checking excludes ‘Smart_Conveyor’ and ‘world/obstacles’ primitives, relying on PoseCostMetric for linear Z-axis constraints.
Robots return to their predefined home configurations after finishing their respective nailing sequences.
